info.name: Zuplo Developer API
info.description:
The Zuplo Developer API (ZAPI) enables developers to programmatically manage API keys, consumers, buckets, tunnels, deployments, metering, audit logs, and more.

Contact Support:
 Name: Zuplo
 Email: support@zuplo.com

request.description: The Zuplo Documentation MCP server

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

request.description: Returns the OpenAPI V3 spec for Zuplo's Developer API

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: Maximum number of results to return (1-100)
Number of results to skip for pagination
Filter by specific action (e.g., 'account.create')
Filter events after this date (ISO 8601 format)
Filter events before this date (ISO 8601 format)
Filter by actor email address or subject identifier
Filter by success status (true for successful, false for failed)

variable.description: (Required) The name of the account. You can find this in your Zuplo Portal under Settings > Project Information.

query.description:
The format of the key to return.
`none`: Key is completely hidden.
`visible`: Key is completely visible.
`masked`: Part of key suffix is visible.
Query by tag. Example usage is `tag.account=foo`, where `account` is the name of the tag property and `foo` is the value.

(Required) The offset of the first item returned in the collection. Default: 0

`masked`: Part of key suffix is visible.

query.description: Query by tag. Example usage is `tag.account=foo`, where `account` is the name of the tag property and `foo` is the value.
(Required) The maximum number of entries to return. If the value exceeds the maximum, then the maximum value will be used. Default: 1000
(Required) The offset of the first item returned in the collection. Default: 0
Include the api key data in the response.
Include the manager invites data in the response.
Include the manager's data in the response.

The format of the key to return.
`none`: Key is completely hidden.
`visible`: Key is completely visible.
`masked`: Part of key suffix is visible.
Filter by email address of key manager.

request.description: Returns basic information about the caller. Supports both API key and JWT authentication. The response always includes the subject identifier (`sub`).
